perturbation.original_text: F. "Licensed Products" shall mean BlackMP Living Water, BlackMP Concentrate, Zezel Probiotic Water, Zayin Sports Water, Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotics™ Water, Gridiron MVP™ and Gridiron MVP™ Concentrate using the Pro Football Legends Logo on the Licensed Products' affixed labels, hang-tags or packaging.
perturbation.changed_text: F. "Endorsed Goods" shall mean BlackMP Living Water, BlackMP Concentrate, Zezel Probiotic Water, Zayin Sports Water, Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotics™ Water, Gridiron MVP™ and Gridiron MVP™ Concentrate using the Pro Football Legends Logo on the Endorsed Goods' affixed labels, hang-tags or packaging.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Licensed Products" to "Endorsed Goods" in Section One, F, creates confusion because the term 'Licensed Products' is used in Section Four, C, concerning remuneration. It is now unclear if the donation applies to 'Endorsed Goods'. This creates an in-text contradiction by using different terms for the same product category without clarification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION FOUR. REMUNERATION C. A *donation of $0.05 per Unit sold of Licensed Products within the Contract Territory payable to the **NFL Alumni Northern California Chapter. Donated amounts will be allocated and dispersed to the Northern California Chapter beginning on the first full quarter [three (3) month period] of the Agreement and continue on a quarterly basis thereafter for the term of this Agreement. Where the following per Unit conversion shall apply for the term of this Agreement: a. (1) Bottle of BlackMP LivingWater = 1 Unit b. (1) 4oz bottle of BlackMPConcentrate = 30 Units c. (1) Bottle of Zezel ProbioticWater = 1 Unit d. (1) Bottle of Zayin Sports Water = 1 Unit e. (1) Bottle Gridiron MVP™ Water= 1 Unit f. (1) Bottle Gridiron CBD H20 Probiotics™ Water = 1 Unit g. (1) 4oz bottle of Gridiron MVP™Concentrate = 30 Units _____________ * The NFLA-NC will donate 15% of the above described proceeds to the NFLA. ** The Company will provide to the NFLA-NC upon request the most recent quarterly sales report of the Company's Licensed Products.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes "Licensed Products" to "Endorsed Goods" in the definition section. Section Four, C, explicitly states that a donation of $0.05 per Unit sold is applicable to 'Licensed Products'. Since 'Licensed Products' is redefined as 'Endorsed Goods', it creates a clear contradiction, because it is now uncertain whether the donation applies to what was previously called 'Licensed Products' and the document uses different terms for the same product category without clarification. This meets the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: National Football League Alumni - Northern California Chapter ("NFLA-NC"), a charitable corporation organized under the laws of California, having its principal office at 1311 Madison Avenue, Redwood CA 94061
perturbation.changed_text: Northern California NFL Alumni ("NFLA-NC"), a charitable corporation organized under the laws of California, having its principal office at 1311 Madison Avenue, Redwood CA 94061
perturbation.explanation: By changing the name "National Football League Alumni - Northern California Chapter" to "Northern California NFL Alumni", the Addendum creates uncertainty regarding whether the NFLA-NC mentioned in Section Four (Remuneration) is the same entity initially described. This use of similar, but not identical, terminology without clarification introduces a potential contradiction, as it's unclear which entity is entitled to the donations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION FOUR. REMUNERATION C. A *donation of $0.05 per Unit sold of Licensed Products within the Contract Territory payable to the **NFL Alumni Northern California Chapter. Donated amounts will be allocated and dispersed to the Northern California Chapter beginning on the first full quarter [three (3) month period] of the Agreement and continue on a quarterly basis thereafter for the term of this Agreement. Where the following per Unit conversion shall apply for the term of this Agreement: a. (1) Bottle of BlackMP LivingWater = 1 Unit b. (1) 4oz bottle of BlackMPConcentrate = 30 Units c. (1) Bottle of Zezel ProbioticWater = 1 Unit d. (1) Bottle of Zayin Sports Water = 1 Unit e. (1) Bottle Gridiron MVP™ Water= 1 Unit f. (1) Bottle Gridiron CBD H20 Probiotics™ Water = 1 Unit g. (1) 4oz bottle of Gridiron MVP™Concentrate = 30 Units _____________ * The NFLA-NC will donate 15% of the above described proceeds to the NFLA. ** The Company will provide to the NFLA-NC upon request the most recent quarterly sales report of the Company's Licensed Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'National Football League Alumni - Northern California Chapter' (NFLA-NC). The changed text shortens this to 'Northern California NFL Alumni' (NFLA-NC). Section Four then refers to 'NFL Alumni Northern California Chapter'. Although all three names are similar and include the abbreviation 'NFLA-NC', the subtle differences create ambiguity and a potential contradiction. It becomes unclear whether the donation is intended for the entity defined in the changed text or for a different entity with a similar name. This directly aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where the contract uses multiple similar terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to potentially conflicting obligations regarding who receives the donation.

perturbation.original_text: Parties agree to the addition of Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotic™ Water to "Licensed Products" as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Parties agree to the addition of Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotic™ Water to "Approved Products" as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Licensed Products” to "Approved Products" and in addition to changing 'Licensed Products' to 'Endorsed Goods' on Section ONE F, creates confusion because both terms are used in Section Four, C, concerning remuneration. It is now unclear if the donation applies to "Approved Products" and "Endorsed Goods", because in this Section only 'Licensed Products' is being mentioned. This creates an in-text contradiction by using different terms for the same product category without clarification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION FOUR. REMUNERATION C. A *donation of $0.05 per Unit sold of Licensed Products within the Contract Territory payable to the **NFL Alumni Northern California Chapter. Donated amounts will be allocated and dispersed to the Northern California Chapter beginning on the first full quarter [three (3) month period] of the Agreement and continue on a quarterly basis thereafter for the term of this Agreement. Where the following per Unit conversion shall apply for the term of this Agreement: a. (1) Bottle of BlackMP LivingWater = 1 Unit b. (1) 4oz bottle of BlackMPConcentrate = 30 Units c. (1) Bottle of Zezel ProbioticWater = 1 Unit d. (1) Bottle of Zayin Sports Water = 1 Unit e. (1) Bottle Gridiron MVP™ Water= 1 Unit f. (1) Bottle Gridiron CBD H20 Probiotics™ Water = 1 Unit g. (1) 4oz bottle of Gridiron MVP™Concentrate = 30 Units _____________ * The NFLA-NC will donate 15% of the above described proceeds to the NFLA. ** The Company will provide to the NFLA-NC upon request the most recent quarterly sales report of the Company's Licensed Products.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces "Licensed Products" with "Approved Products". The contradicted text in Section Four. C. refers specifically to a donation based on sales of "Licensed Products". Because of the change, it is ambiguous whether the donation applies to "Approved Products" which are now considered different from 'Licensed Products', creating a direct contradiction regarding the remuneration terms and creating potential unenforceability. This aligns with the definition of misaligned terminology creating in-text contradiction.
